title: leucas zeylanica var. walkeri
intro: Leucas zeylanica var. walkeri, also known as the Sri Lankan hyptis or wild basil, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is said to possess various health benefits, including relief from fever, cold, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Leucas zeylanica var. walkeri is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
